F.crn Por-rrrcAr- PnACTrcEs C orvrMrssroN
-ll8 J Street o Suite 610 o Sacranrento. CA 9581-l-2-129
t9l6) -322-5660 r Fax (916) -122-0886
September 30, 2010
Richard Holst
Assistant Court Executive Officer
Butte County Superior Court
1 Court Street
Oroville, CA 96965
RE: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-10-135
Dear Mr. Holst:
This letter is in response to your reqLlest on behalf of the Butte County Superior Court
("Court"), fbr advice regarding the conflict-of-interest code provisions of the Political Refbrm
Act (the "Act").1
QUESTIONS
1. Is the Court the appropriate code reviewing body for the Butte County Law Library
Board of Tmstees?
2. [s the Court the appropriate code reviewing body for the Butte County Board of
Parole Commissioners?
CONCLUSIONS
1. No, the Butte County Board of Supervisors is the code reviewing body for the Butte
County Law Library Board of Trustees.
2. No, the Butte County Board of Supervisors is the code reviewing body for the Butte
County Board of Parole Commissioners.
| 'Ihe Political Retirrm Act is contained in Government Code Sections tt 100() throu-eh 91014. .,\ll statutory
ret'erences are to the (iovernment Code unless otherwise indicated.'[he regulations of the FairPolitical Practices
CommissionarecontainedinSections lllll0through llt997of Title2of theCalitirrniaCodeof Regulations.All
re-uulakrry ret'erences are to'fitle 2. Division 6 olthe Califirrnia Code of Regulations unless otherwise indicate<l.
";;.b


File No. A-10-135
Page No. 2
FACTS
The Court is currently in the process of revising its conflict-of-interest code (code).
Appendix B of the code lists the Law Library Board of Tn stees and the Board of parole
Commissioners as committees or commissions for which the members mllst file Statements of
Economic Interest (Form 700) with the Court. This was a requirement prior to the transfer of the
Court from County control to the exclusive control of the Staie Judicial Branch.
Neither the Law Library Board of Trustees nor the Board of parole Commissioners are
agencies of the judicial branch of govemment. As such, the Court would like to amend its
conflict-of-interest code to remove both agencies from its code.
Other areas of law grant the power of appointment of Trustees to the County Board of
Supervisors. The Law Library Board of Trustees functions independently of the Court; and its
jurisdiction is wholly within Butte County. The Court does not have the po*., to appoint
additional members to the Law Library Board of Trustees, nor does it have control, supervision,
or direction over the actions carried out by the Trustees.
The Board of Parole Commissioners in Butte County consists of the Sheriff (or his or her
designee), the Chief Probation officer (or his or her designee) and a member of the public
selected by the Presiding Judge of the Court. Although imember of the Board of parole
Commissioners is appointed by the Court, the Board of Parole Commissioners acts
independently of the Court. The establishment of rules and regulations governing a prisoner,s
eligibility for parole and subsequent release is carried out with no superiision Uyltre Court, and
the Board of Parole Commissioners acts autonomously from the Court in its respective duties.
The Court has no direct control or supervision over thl decisions. As such, the Court believes
that the Board of Parole Commissioners is an agency with jurisdiction wholly within Butte
County and that the appropriate filing agency is the County Board of Supervisors.
In cooperation with Butte County, you seek advice addressing the appropriate code
reviewing body for both agencies.
ANALYSIS
The Act, specifically Section 87300, requires every state and local government agency to
adopt a conflict-of-interest code. The code must enumerate the positions Jithin the agericy that
involve the making or participation in the making of decisions that may foreseeably have a
material financial effect on any financial interest held by the person filiing the posilion. Further,
the code specifies which financial interests must be disClosedby persons [olding those positions
designated in the code, all with a view to avoiding conflicts of inierest. (Sections g7300-g7302.)
The issue addressed in your request for advice is whether the Butte County Superior Court
should be the code reviewing body for the Law Library Board of Trustees-and^Board of parole
Commissioners, or if the Butte County Board of Supeivisors should be the code reviewing body.


File No. A-10-135
Page No. 3
section 82011 defines "code reviewing body" to mean the following:
"(b) The board of supervisors, with respect to the conflict-of-interest code of
any county agency other than the board of supervisors, or any agency of the
jurdicial branch of government, and of any local government agency, other
than a city agency, with jurisdiction wholly within the county . . ..,;
"(g) The Chief Justice of California, the administrative presiding judges of
the courts of appeal, and the presiding judges of superior and municipal
courts, or their designees, with respect to the conflict-of-interest code of any
agency of the judicial branch of govemment subject to the immediate
administrative supervision of the court . . .."
Under Section 82011(b), the board of supervisors is the code reviewing body with respect
to the conflict-of-interest code of any local government agency, other than u.ity agency, with
jurisdiction wholly within the county. Therefore, it is the duty of the board of supervisors to
review and approve the conflict-of-interest code for any agency for which it is the code
reviewing body. The facts you provided, and a review of California Penal Code Section 3075
(goveming county boards of parole commissioners) and California Business and professions
Code Sections 6340-6350 (goveming county law libraries), indicate that the Butte County Board
of Parole Commissioners and the Butte County Law Library Board of Trustees are county
agencies, rather than judicial branch agencies subject to the immediate administrative
supervision of the Butte County Superior Court. Therefore, the Butte County Board of
Supervisors is the code reviewing body for county agencies and local government agencies with
jurisdiction wholly within the county, including the Board of Parole Commissioners and the Law
Library Board of Trustees.
Please be aware that whether a separate code is established or the officials are included in
Butte County's code is a determination to be made by the Board of Supervisors, the code
reviewing body. (Sections 87301 and 820111 see also Regulation 1g329.5.)
If you have any other questions regarding this matter or need technical assistance
regarding the establishment or amendment of a code, please contact the Technical Assistance
Division at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
Sarah Olson
Political Reform Consultant
SO:jgl
By:
